content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print first ten multiples of NNN.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain a single integer NNN.


Output Format

Output on a single line, ten space separated numbers - first ten multiples of NNN.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print ten space‑separated integers – the first ten multiples of N – on a single line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1 and N=2. The first ten multiples of 2 are printed.
T=1 and N=5. The output lists 5,10,…,50.
Three test cases: N=1, N=10 and N=100. Each line shows the first ten multiples of the respective N.

solution.approach: The problem is straightforward: for each given N we need to output N,2N,…,10N. We read the number of test cases T, then iterate T times. For each N we generate the ten multiples using a simple for‑loop from 1 to 10, appending each product to a StringBuilder separated by spaces. Finally we print each test case result on its own line. The algorithm is O(T) time (10 operations per test case) and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space per test case (output buffer excluded).
